title: Eesha Jagdhane
text:
Skills

Programming & Data: SQL, Python, Pandas, PostgreSQL, Snowflake, AWS Athena, Excel
Visualization & Tools: Tableau, Tableau Prep, PowerPoint
Analytics & Business Applications: Data Validation, Reporting Automation, Dashboard Development, KPI Tracking, Process Improvement, Business Analysis
Modeling & Statistics: A/B Testing, Hypothesis Testing, Regression, Forecasting, Exploratory Data Analysis
Collaboration & Project Tools: Git, GitHub, JIRA, Stakeholder Collaboration, Documentation
Languages: English, Marathi, Hindi

title: Eesha Jagdhane
text:
Interests
I’m interested in how data can be used not just to analyze what happened, but to improve what happens next. My interests lie in business analytics, machine learning, and using data to solve real operational and strategic problems. I enjoy connecting technical work with human decision-making, whether that means building better processes, uncovering insights, or translating complexity into something clear and useful.

title: Eesha Jagdhane
text:
Hobbies
Outside of academics and work, I love cooking and baking and have been doing both since sixth grade. I am a big foodie and enjoy trying new restaurants and dishes, especially around San Diego. Some of my favorite spots are Seneca, Callie, Taco Stand, Din Tai Fung, and Ramen Nagi.
Here are some pictures of what I have cooked and baked:
  
  
I also enjoy reading, especially fiction and mystery, though I am usually open to almost anything. Lately, I have been drawn to books that help me step away from work and assignments for a bit.
When I have the time, I enjoy painting and sketching as well. I actually started college as a studio arts major before eventually switching to finance and data analytics, so creativity has always been an important part of who I am.
I am also a big fan of video games. I have played all the Uncharted games, play Call of Duty occasionally, and especially enjoy co-op adventure games like It Takes Two, Split Fiction, Mario Kart, and Overcooked.

title: World Cup Winner Prediction
text:
My pick: France!
MGTA 495 — Marketing Analytics
The team is a tournament-experienced squad that made it to the semi-finals during the last FIFA World Cup. They have elite players like Kylian Mbappé, Ousmane Dembélé, and Mike Maignan.

title: World Cup winner prediction
text:
Why France is my pick

Elite depth: France has high-level talent in every area of the field, not just a few star players.
Big-game experience: They have consistently made deep tournament runs, so the squad knows how to handle knockout pressure.
Strong chemistry: Many of their players are used to playing in demanding, high-stakes matches together.
Tactical flexibility: France can win in different ways, whether through counterattacks, physicality, or controlling key moments.
Match-winners: They have players who can change a game instantly with one run, pass, or finish.

title: World Cup Winner Prediction
text:
Caveats


Injuries — Even one key injury can completely change a team’s chances.




Tournament draw — A difficult bracket path can make even the best team vulnerable.




Knockout variance — One bad performance, one mistake, or one unlucky moment can end a run.


These are the biggest reasons predictions should be treated as probabilities, not guarantees.
